title: 6 Uttarakhand districts face heatwave conditions: Rain expected in 5 districts; school timings changed in N... - Bhaskar English
full_text:
- Uttarakhand Rain Alert | Heatwave In 6 Districts; Nainital School Timings Change
6 Uttarakhand districts face heatwave conditions:Rain expected in 5 districts; school timings changed in Nainital
- Copy link
A heatwave alert has been issued in 6 districts of Uttarakhand, while 5 districts are likely to receive rain with thunder and lightning. Tourists visiting hill stations such as Mussoorie, Nainital and Lansdowne to escape the heat are also facing strong sunshine and rising temperatures. The temperature in these hill towns has crossed 30 °C. Due to the heat, school timings in Nainital have been changed from May 22 until further orders.
According to the Meteorological Department, heatwave conditions may occur in low-lying areas near Dehradun, Haridwar, Udham Singh Nagar, Nainital, Pauri and Uttarkashi districts.
At the same time, light rain with thunder and lightning is expected in Uttarkashi, Rudraprayag, Chamoli, Bageshwar and Pithoragarh districts. Strong winds at a speed of 40 to 50 kmph and lightning strikes may also occur in these areas.
The Health Department has issued an advisory due to the intense heat. People have been advised to avoid going out in the sun between 12 pm and 4 pm, drink enough water and step outside only if necessary. Children, elderly people and outdoor workers have been asked to take extra care.
Temperature in major cities of the state
Source: IMD
Even in hilly areas, heat is more than normal
Hilly regions are also experiencing more heat than usual this time. According to IMD, in the last 48 hours, the maximum temperature recorded in Nainital was 31 °C and in Mussoorie was 30 °C.
In Lansdowne, 31C, Chakrata, 32 °C, Kausani, 28 °C, and Joshimath, 27 °C temperatures were recorded. Due to strong sunlight during the day, tourists also appeared troubled, although the weather was somewhat relieved in the evening. Severe Heat and Heatwave Conditions in Dehradun
In the capital Dehradun, the weather will remain clear on Thursday, and scorching sun is expected throughout the day. According to the Meteorological Department, the maximum temperature in the city could reach up to 42C, which will make the effect of heat and humidity feel much more intense. Heatwave-like conditions are expected to persist during the afternoon.
Nainital: school timings changed now
Nainital Chief Education Officer Govind Ram Jaiswal has issued orders to change the timings of government, non-government, aided, and private schools operating in the plain areas of Haldwani, Ramnagar, and Kotabagh, considering the heat and the health of students.
According to the order, from May 22 until further orders, morning prayers in schools will be at 6:45 AM, while classes will be conducted only from 7 AM to 11:30 AM. Schools in hilly areas have been excluded from this order.
Daily reports being sought from all districts
Considering the intense heat and the threat of heatwave, the state government has put the health department on alert mode. Daily reports of patients affected by heatwave and sunstroke are being sought from all districts.
Health Secretary Sachin Kurve said that preventing deaths due to heatstroke is the government's priority. Instructions have been given to create separate rooms for heatwave patients in district and sub-district hospitals. Hospitals have been asked to keep cooling systems, ice packs, cold fluids, ORS, and essential medicines available.
Emphasis has been placed on implementing the 'cool first, then transport' protocol for serious patients. The health department has placed children, pregnant women, the elderly, and outdoor laborers in the high-risk category.
Weather conditions a day earlier
Uttarakhand experienced the effects of severe heat and heatwave on Thursday. Dehradun remained the hottest city in the state, where the temperature was recorded at around 42C by afternoon. In Haridwar, the temperature was 41C, in Rudrapur and Pantnagar it was 41C, while in Haldwani, Kashipur, and Kotdwar it was recorded around 40C. Heatwave-like conditions persisted in the plains from morning due to strong sunlight and hot winds.
By afternoon, the effect of heat and humidity further increased. People appeared troubled due to the scorching sun. Many people were seen carrying umbrellas to avoid the sun, while some had covered their heads and faces with cloth. People driving vehicles on the roads also appeared troubled by the strong hot winds and heat.
Meanwhile, tourists arrived in Nainital to escape the heat of the plains, where crowds of people were seen around Naini Lake.

title: Weather Today: Severe Heatwave Grinds Delhi, UP And Punjab; Yellow Alert Issued For Uttarakhand | Check Updates - The Daily Jagran
full_text:
As the heatwave conditions continue to persist, there seems to be no respite in the night hours either, as Delhi logged a minimum temperature of 31.9 degrees Celsius on Thursday, marking the season’s first “warm night," the Indian Meteorological Department (IMD) reported.
The minimum temperature recorded at Safdarjung, Delhi’s base weather station, was five degrees above normal, making it the warmest night for Delhi in May in the last 14 years. According to IMD data, the warmest night in May was recorded on May 27, 2012, when the minimum temperature was 34.2 degrees Celsius.
The maximum temperature, meanwhile, was 43.6 degrees Celsius, 3.4 degrees Celsius above normal. The maximum peaked at other stations, with Ridge recording 45.3 degrees Celsius, as isolated heatwave conditions continued for a fourth day in a row. IMD extended its “orange” alert for heatwaves by a day, until May 27, on that day.
Escalating Temperatures Across States
UP has been experiencing a heatwave condition for nearly two weeks. In particular, Banda, Jhansi, Agra, Prayagraj, Varanasi, and surrounding districts are setting record maximum temperatures every day.
Prayagraj topped the list at 46.6 degrees Celsius, followed by Varanasi at 45.6 degrees Celsius; Hamirpur at 45.2 degrees Celsius; Sultanpur at 45.2 degrees Celsius; Orai at 45 degrees Celsius; and Agra at 44.8 degrees Celsius. The Meteorological Department has issued a severe heatwave alert for the next two days.
In Punjab's Faridkot, the temperature was recorded at around 45.9 degrees Celsius. Dehri Rohtas in Bihar was 44.6 degrees Celsius. A heat wave warning has been issued for Arwal, Bhojpur, Buxar, Aurangabad, Bhabua, and Rohtas. Patna District Magistrate Dr. Tyagarajan SM has completely suspended all educational activities in all public and private schools up to the fifth grade.
Temperature in several areas of Jharkhand was recorded at around 44 degrees Celsius. The Jharkhand Health Department has directed all civil surgeons and health officials to ensure that emergency medical facilities in hospitals are fully operational.
Yellow Alert Issued In Uttarakhand
According to the IMD, a yellow alert has been issued for lightning and strong winds of up to 50 km/h in some parts of Uttarkashi, Tehri, Rudraprayag, Chamoli, Bageshwar, and Pithoragarh districts, including Dehradun, on May 22. Furthermore, a drop of two to four degrees in maximum temperatures across the state is predicted over the next three days.
Contrasting Weather Patterns In West Bengal
The heat wave is likely to continue in the Gangetic South Bengal districts like Kolkata, Howrah, and Hooghly. The maximum temperature in Kolkata may reach 35 to 36 degrees Celsius. The minimum temperature may also be around 28 degrees Celsius. The humidity level in the air may reach 70 to 85 percent on this day. Although there is a possibility of light rain this afternoon.
Unlike South Bengal, North Bengal is getting drenched in rain. There is a possibility of heavy rainfall in Darjeeling, Jalpaiguri, and Alipurduar in the north. These districts may receive rainfall ranging from 7 to 20 centimeters. The Meteorological Office has predicted that the rainfall will continue till next Tuesday.
